info.title: Gmail API
info.description: Vendored, trimmed OpenAPI 3.0.3 spec for the Gmail v1 API. Reads, searches, labels, and sends mail for the connected account. Use userId 'me' everywhere — it means the account whose OAuth grant is injected. Which operations are permitted depends on the permission level granted at connect time: reading needs gmail.readonly (or gmail.modify), label changes need gmail.modify, sending needs gmail.send — a call outside the granted level fails with HTTP 403. Authorization is handled by the caller (a bearer OAuth2 access token); no auth is declared in this spec.

get.description: Returns the connected account's email address and mailbox counters. Cheap way to discover which mailbox this connection acts as. Requires read access (gmail.readonly or gmail.modify).

Lists message ids matching a Gmail search query (same syntax as the Gmail search box, e.g. 'from:billing@acme.com has:attachment newer_than:7d'). Returns ids only — fetch each message with messages_get. Requires read access (gmail.readonly or gmail.modify).

parameters.name: q
parameters.description: Gmail search query, e.g. 'from:someone@example.com subject:invoice is:unread'. Omit to list everything.

parameters.name: labelIds
parameters.description: Only return messages with ALL of these label ids (e.g. 'INBOX', 'UNREAD', or a user label id from labels_list). Repeat the parameter for multiple labels.

parameters.name: maxResults
parameters.description: Maximum messages to return per page (default 100, max 500).

parameters.name: pageToken
parameters.description: Page token from a previous messages_list response.

parameters.name: includeSpamTrash
parameters.description: Include messages from SPAM and TRASH (default false).

get.description: Returns a message. format=metadata (with metadataHeaders=From,To,Subject,Date) is the cheap way to triage; format=full returns the MIME tree whose text parts carry base64url-encoded content in body.data; attachments larger than a few KB carry an attachmentId instead — fetch the bytes with messages_attachments_get. Requires read access (gmail.readonly or gmail.modify).

parameters.name: format
parameters.description: How much of the message to return: minimal (ids + labels only), metadata (headers, no body), full (parsed MIME tree, default), raw (entire RFC 2822 message, base64url in the 'raw' field).

parameters.name: metadataHeaders
parameters.description: With format=metadata: only include these headers, e.g. 'From', 'To', 'Subject', 'Date'. Repeat for multiple.

get.description: Returns an attachment's bytes as base64url in the 'data' field. Get the attachmentId from the message's MIME part (messages_get with format=full). Requires read access (gmail.readonly or gmail.modify).

parameters.name: messageId
parameters.description: The id of the message containing the attachment.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The attachment id from the message part's body.attachmentId.

post.summary: Add or remove labels on a message
post.description: Adds and/or removes label ids on a message — this is how mail is marked read (remove 'UNREAD'), archived (remove 'INBOX'), or filed under a user label (add its id from labels_list). Requires the 'Manage labels' permission (gmail.modify).

Sends an email as the connected account. The body's 'raw' field is a complete RFC 2822 message (headers + blank line + body), base64url-encoded — e.g. base64url('To: a@b.com
Subject: Hi
Content-Type: text/plain; charset=utf-8

Hello'). Set threadId to reply within an existing thread (also keep the Subject and add In-Reply-To/References headers for correct threading). Requires the 'Send mail' permission (gmail.send).

get.description: Lists thread ids matching a Gmail search query — same query syntax as messages_list, but grouped by conversation. Requires read access (gmail.readonly or gmail.modify).

parameters.name: labelIds
parameters.description: Only return threads with ALL of these label ids.

parameters.name: maxResults
parameters.description: Maximum threads to return per page (default 100, max 500).

parameters.name: pageToken
parameters.description: Page token from a previous threads_list response.

get.description: Returns a conversation with all its messages, oldest first. Use format=metadata for a cheap overview of who said what when. Requires read access (gmail.readonly or gmail.modify).

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The thread id (from threads_list or a message's threadId).

parameters.name: format
parameters.description: How much of each message to return: minimal, metadata, or full (default).

get.description: Lists all labels in the mailbox — system labels (INBOX, UNREAD, STARRED, SENT, TRASH, SPAM) and user labels with their ids, which messages_modify and the labelIds filters take. Requires read access (gmail.readonly or gmail.modify).

userId.name: userId
userId.description: The mailbox to act on. Always pass 'me' — the account whose OAuth grant this connection carries.

messagesTotal.description: Total number of messages in the mailbox.

threadsTotal.description: Total number of threads in the mailbox.

messages.description: Matching messages — id and threadId only; fetch details with messages_get.

nextPageToken.description: Token for the next page, absent on the last page.

threadId.description: The id of the thread the message belongs to.
The id of the thread the message belongs to.

labelIds.description: Label ids applied to this message (e.g. INBOX, UNREAD).

snippet.description: A short plain-text preview of the message.

internalDate.description: Epoch ms timestamp Gmail received the message, as a string.

raw.description: With format=raw: the entire RFC 2822 message, base64url-encoded.

MessagePart.description: One MIME part of a message. multipart/* parts nest children under 'parts'; leaf text parts carry base64url content in body.data; attachment parts carry body.attachmentId instead.

partId.description: The part's id within the message.

filename.description: The attachment filename; empty for non-attachment parts.

headers.description: The part's headers (for the top-level payload: From, To, Subject, Date, …).

parts.description: Child parts, for multipart/* MIME types.

MessagePartBody.description: The body of a MIME part.

attachmentId.description: Present when the body is stored as a separate attachment — fetch it with messages_attachments_get.

data.description: The body bytes, base64url-encoded. Absent when attachmentId is set.

addLabelIds.description: Label ids to add (e.g. a user label id, or 'STARRED').

removeLabelIds.description: Label ids to remove (e.g. 'UNREAD' to mark read, 'INBOX' to archive).

raw.description: The complete RFC 2822 message (headers, blank line, body), base64url-encoded.

threadId.description: Send as a reply within this existing thread (pair with In-Reply-To/References headers and the same Subject).

threads.description: Matching threads — id and snippet only; fetch details with threads_get.

nextPageToken.description: Token for the next page, absent on the last page.

snippet.description: A short preview of the latest message.

id.description: The label id — what messages_modify and labelIds filters take.
